name: Mosquito
evidence: the Glasflügel 303 Mosquito. Our wing area 9.85 m²; the article's 9.86 m². One square centimetre is not a coincidence — but the names share neither a word nor a number, so no rule will ever reach it.

name: LS-8-15
evidence: Rolladen-Schneider LS8. Wikipedia's search offers the SCHLEICHER K 8 first, on their shared digit 8, and it is a glider, so a description check waves it through. The right article is the second hit. A human read both.

name: LS-8-18
evidence: as LS-8-15: the item is the AIRCRAFT, and the 18 m tips are a configuration of it. The span stays empty — the article states the LS8's, not the LS8-18's.

name: Antares 18S
evidence: Lange Antares. The item is right and the wing area disagrees (10.97 m² against the article's 12.6) because the article describes the 20E. A failure of CONFIGURATION, which no area check can tell from a failure of IDENTITY.

name: Phoebus C
evidence: Bölkow Phoebus. Same shape: the item is right, and the C's wing is larger than the one the article measures (14.06 m² against 13.16).

name: VT-116
evidence: Orličan VT-16 Orlík — and the article's Variants section names the VT-116 Orlík II explicitly, with more than 50 built. The item is the aircraft; the article gives no span for the VT-116, so ours stays empty. Checked by a human against the article, 2026-07-14.
